author: @hernancorre199
text:
LA CAÑA DE BAMBÚ

Oy hablare de la caña de bambú
La caña de bambú  es sagrada y poderosísima  

"Y miré la altura de la casa alrededor: los cimientos de las cámaras eran una caña entera de seis codos de grandor". (Vers. 8; Cap. 41; Ezequiel).

"Y el que hablaba conmigo, tenía una medida de una caña de oro para medir la ciudad, y sus puertas y su muro". (Vers. 15, Cap. 21, Apocalipsis).

"Y llevome allí, y he aquí un varón, cuyo aspecto era como aspecto de metal, y tenía un cordel de lino en su mano, y una caña de medir: y él estaba a la puerta". (Vers. 3; Cap.  Ezequiel).

La caña Sagrada es el cetro de Nostros los  Venerables Maestros Mamás de  la poderosa y maravillosa y misteriosa y Luminosa antigua Sierra Nevada de Santa Marta Colombia maestros de la Fraternidad Blanca.

En la caña sagrada se registra el ascenso o el descenso del fuego sagrado.

Dentro de la caña está toda la sabiduría del río Eufrates.

 Dentro de la caña está toda la sabiduría de los cuatro ríos del Edén.

La caña sagrada de oro puro representa exactamente nuestra columna espinal.

En el centro de nuestra médula espinal existe un fino canal medular. Ese fino canal medular es el canal de Susumná.

 En el centro del canal de Susumná existe un hilo que corre a lo largo de la médula espinal.

 Por ese fino hilo nervioso asciende el Kundalini, desde el coxis hasta el entrecejo siguiendo el curso medular.

 Nuestra columna espinal tiene treinta y tres vértebras, las cuales son llamadas en ocultismo cañones.

- Los treinta y tres cañones, representan los treinta y tres grados esotéricos de la masonería oculta.

 El Kundalini se despierta practicando magia-sexual.

El Kundalini es el fuego sagrado.

El Kundalini se halla encerrado en una bolsa membranosa, situada en el hueso coxígeo.

 Con la magia-sexual, el Kundalini entra en actividad, rompe la bolsa membranosa en que se halla encerrado, y entra en el canal medular por un orificio o puerta situada en la parte inferior de la médula.

Esta puerta medular permanece cerrada en personas comunes y corrientes.

 Los vapores seminales permiten entonces al ángel gobernador de los elementales de los cedros, abrir esa puerta, para que nuestra culebra ígnea entre por allí...

 El fuego va subiendo lentamente, de acuerdo con los méritos del corazón.

Cada una de nuestras treinta y tres cámaras sagradas representa determinados poderes cósmicos, y determinadas sumas de valores de santidad.

 El ángel gobernador de todos los elementales de los manzanos, va abriendo las cámaras santas de nuestra columna espinal, conforme practicamos magia-sexual y nos santificamos.

 En el semen existe un átomo Angélico que gobierna a nuestros vapores seminales.

 Ese átomo angélico eleva los vapores de nuestro semen hacia el canal medular; para que el ángel de los cedros del bosque lo utilice para abrir la puerta inferior de la médula, a fin de que la divina princesa del Kundalini entre por allí.

Ahora bien,  boy a dar la técnica completamente gratis: ¿quiere usted saber la clave para convertirse en un Dios?
Esta es la fórmula: "Penetrar  el miembro viril en la vagina, y retirarse sin derramar el semen". Con este secreto tu  te convertirás en un Dios  extremadamente poderoso omnisciente omnipotente siempre y cuando te vuelvas tu  un santo perfecto.

IIIIAAAAOOOO, es el Mantram que se debe vocalizar durante este trance de magia-sexual diaria, así: Iiiiiiiiiiii Aaaaaaaaaa Ooooooooooo.




Por ello las puertas del templo de Salomón se construyeron con cedros del Líbano.

En la palabra LÍBANO se halla encerrada el I.A.O., que permite al ángel de los cedros del bosque abrir la puerta de la médula espinal, cuando practicamos magia-sexual.

I.A.O. es el Mantram de la magia-sexual.

La pronunciación correcta de este Mantram es vocalizando cada letra por separado y alargando el sonido de cada vocal.

 El Mantram I.A.O. se debe vocalizar durante los trances de la magia-sexual para despertar nuestro fuego sagrado.

En nuestra columna espinal existen siete "NADIS", o centros ocultos, simbolizados por los siete nudos de la caña de bambú.

 Nuestra columna espinal, verdaderamente tiene la forma de una caña de bambú con sus siete nudos.

 Los rituales del primero, segundo y hasta tercer grado, con los cuales oficiamos los Gnósticos, pertenecen a la caña.

Nuestra columna espinal tiene dos orificios, uno inferior y uno superior.

 El orificio inferior, es la puerta de entrada a la médula, y el superior, situado en la parte superior del cráneo, es la puerta de salida de la médula. Por allí desciende la fuerza terrible de las Jerarquías, junto con el silbido del FOHAT, por entre las profundidades de nuestra caña, para hacer subir el fuego sagrado cuando nos ganamos un cañón espinal.

 Entonces se abre una puerta ante nosotros y un Maestro nos dice: entra.

 Y entramos en un patio, y en un templo para recibir el grado, los símbolos y la fiesta.

 Estas son las fiestas de los templos y las fiestas de los dioses.

author: @nedlyest
text: Those old motors rarely actually go bad, check for spark. If there isn't any pull the pull cord off, the stator should be situated above the fly wheel. There should be a bit of metal on the flywheel that almost connects to the stator, this gets crusty from sitting  and doesn't make a good connection resulting in no spark. Clean that up with some Emory cloth. 50% if the time that's all that's wrong. The other part of the time the carb is clogged from old fuel that's has been sitting for forever. Just pop it of and clean it out. You would be surprised how many mowers/weedwhacker go into the garbage because people just don't maintenance them.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

author: @dcbourbonireland
text: Can u confirm my maths 1 liter of HHO makes ~4 watts of power. In a typical single 250cc each piston compression explosion cycle in that engine will need ~1 watt of power. Typically in electrolysis it takes ~135 watts of electric power to make 1 liter of HHO. If my maths are correct do you think you can make HHO or hydrogen from aluminium system that can can give us HHO for something like 2 watts input power to 4 watt output power or will it be more like 50 watts input power to give 4 watts output power . Giving us a useful reduction in input power to make HHO or hydrogen is still useful especially with solar or end power input power to drive internal combustion engines

author: @Stelios.Posantzis
text: Part of the reason - I think - we live in a throwaway society (well, there's tons of reasons actually) is that people do not understand the inherent value of things, i.e. the value that lies within their latent capacity to serve a function. People throw away things because they don't know any better e.g. when they stop fulfilling their function, when they do not function as well as they used to, when something else comes out that functions better, when people have no  need for that function etc. So when the capacity to function goes away or diminishes, the average bloke or gal, surely does not understand the reason why this is the case and probably cannot be bothered to fix it (because they don't understand why it broke in the first place). If they did understand, they would see the value of the object past it's temporary status and they would understand what it would take to restore its capacity and not only that but see other potential functions based on its internal capacities and even many other uses for the same function. So the answer for me is more science, and in particular more physics, in compulsory education for everyone even for those people who dislike or abhor science. The trick is to get people to love the subject first and secondly to see its usefulness because, let's face it, teenagers are not always interested in practical things. Science is an alien thing to try to teach to someone uninitiated and teaching it involves a certain element of magic. The teachers job is to find that magic first and then find a way to convey it. Of course displaying this magic is not the goal, the goal is to instill in the student the desire to understand it and thus acquire it i.e. the real magic is to get the student to want to know it and to get to know it  We should campaign for more science teachers and especially more gifted teachers! Understanding breeds respect.

author: @stevesimpson5994
text:
The problem is you're fighting the system.  The world is dominated by economic theory, which dictates indefinite growth as the mechanism for future prosperity.  Consumerism is part of this fiction.  Conversation, reuse, and recycling is anathema.


Sheeple buy into this myth and think it strange to repurpose things intended for landfill.


Here's an example close to home.


I'm looking at an old upright piano in my lounge.  It's 100 years old, well out of tune, and has one broken string, but apart from that it's ok.  


Economically, it's a stupid idea to do anything other than take it to the tip.  If I payed a tuner, I'd expect a substantial bill because the whole piano would require tuning several times over before it settled.  And after all that effort it'd still never sound as good as modern piano due to improvements in design.


So, I could try tuning it myself.  I'd need to buy a few cheap tools and watch a few youtube videos.  I would not be able to do as good a job as a tuner and it would take me days because I'm inexperienced.


Would it be worth it?


Absolutely not from a financial perspective.  But, for my family we'd be able to enjoy a good sing-a-long.


Will I do this?


Don't know.  There's many things vying for my time.  Tinkering with ancient pianos has to compete with other interests.  If I buy a new piano, "I won't have to worry"  So what's it to be??

K.

author: @johnwilson3668
text:
I have an engineering workshop in an outbuilding, milling machines ,lathes and so on .I am currently making a wind turbine generator from scratch . It disappoints me 
why people no longer wish to work with their hands any more. They seem to look down on it has something distasteful. In the 1960s most people refurbished items or made them 
from scratch because they could not afford a new one. It is all lost because they are spoilt. I am looking foward to your follow up videos

replies.author: @johnwilson3668
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering I know  a few  exceptions  myself   . What I was saying that in the 60's ,I remember all the neighbours doing work on cars ,repairing fences ,painting 
their houses . I did my own block paving, Built a brick porch. Now there are only two other people in my street I see doing large projects. All the rest use tradesmen.
I designed and build a section of decking last year for a neighbour and he assisted me . I said you can do the other section yourself  now you know how to do it . He said he was not 
confidant enough to do it on his own and this person had a university degree. I would be surprised if Luke didn't turn out the way he has because he has got you to inspire him, like you 
inspire all the rest of us. Lets hope that more people will have a go at making projects

author: @Justin-ng4zg
text: Engineering explained the issues of burning hydrogen in an ice engine. Burning Hydrogen will dump more steam and water into the crankshaft (not good for lubrication.)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: for sure mate - but that's not a reason not to do it- it's a reason to do it and try and overcome any problems that arise - that is what exploring is about - otherwise you end up doing nothing

author: @dt-tv4fs
text: If you are in for a challenge (and I know you are. . .) study the GEET processor. GEET is a strange and proven working carburetor for gasoline engines that changes fuel to plasma fuel. I built one, I can prove it with photos. I first encountered in 2001 and later found someone who proved it could work better .  . . WAY better. . .than what it did on my 3HP lawnmower engine. I witnessed thru video this processor using 50% water, 50% gasoline to drive a delivery van around the demonstration area parking lot without a gas tank. The fuel mixture came from Medical Intravenous bags set to feed this carburetor and run while small groups of audience sat inside the van as it went around the block. Many stories, there are videos on Utube with enough info, (I think) because i just looked up whether the topic was blocked and they exist as early as 2 years ago. If more info is desired i will do my best to provide. I am serious about this. Dan

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I know GEET mate - I have the bits laying around - damn - did that need a spoiler alert lol

author: @m3sca1
text: I've been playing with a bacterial fuel cell that eats ammonium and produces good slow burn gas. Soil bacteria added to a cell...might be a good gas for your motor.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: interesting - cheers mate

replies.author: @m3sca1
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering also growing a substrate to support itself(neg electrode) and erupting bubbles in a pop from what look like hydrothermal smokers. Very very interesting. Use some rods to gather electric bacteria around the rods and use whatever turns up. I'm going for 10 cells to make 5v and see what happens...pulled 20mA from a single cell. It will also recover quickly from short circuit.

First the LTO cases for your batteries, now this generator.  I love your re-use/re-purpose/upcycle mentality.  There is a man in Germany who's channel is all about that. author: @jtpinion4294
text: Hello and good evening to you. Very much enjoy watching your videos as they're always presented in an upbeat and enthusiastic way, but not in that kind of over the top way, which is very refreshing. That is definitely a beefy generator, nice one for getting it free. Very glad to see others reusing other people's scrap. I  have to be careful what I bring home because I'm a tiny bit of a hoarder and have all these ideas but get distracted and tend to over think, and rethink and rethink again about what project I want to do. Although saying that, I have now had more time and energy to actually make some things due to the lowdown. I have made a stand for a bicycle for exercising and intend to fit a generator to the back wheel to put some use to the exercise. Currently working on an ebike as I've rewired the motor from Star to Delta, this is about 6 years in the making lol. Anyways, your videos are very inspiring and always refer to them when I need to clarify things. Thank you very much and looking forward to see what happens with your generator project. Take care. John.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Hiya mate - thanks for taking the time to post - it is a good time for sure - me I tend to do and make mistakes and redo - not the most efficient for sure - but it is fun lol

author: @69waveydavey
text: I saw it happening in the 90's/early 2000's. As a car mechanic, people would just run around in what they had for a few years and spend a few hundred pounds getting it MOT'd and serviced every year. Now they're loathed to spend anything but continually spend on them on lease and at the end of 3 years don't even own the thing. I've always run old bangers, I've had my current one since 2012, it's probably cost me less than a grand in that time, not counting tax and insurance of course. We have to go back to that way of doing things like you say, it's the only way if everybody wants a telly, car, washing machine etc.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I think it's the difference between what we think we want and what it is we actually need - so much stuff comes with added bells and whistles no one actually uses - but we get sold it on the bells and whistles - like phones - oddly enough I use mine to make phone calls - I don't care what the camera is like

replies.author: @69waveydavey
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering I have got in a few cars to MOT them recently and literally havn't been able to start them without having to ask. It's ridiculous. I can spend longer looking for the mileage or the bonnet pull than actually doing the job.

author: @tinkmarshino
text: I agree whole hearted with you.. I hardly ever throw things away.. always fix what I have ( though these days if you buy something new you can figure your going to have to fix it by years end) When something is broken and there is no fixing it.. well there are parts and pieces that can be used down the road to fix, build or repair other thing.. I actually have a generator kind of like your on a shelf in my garage I got the same way.. I haven't gotten a chance yet to get to it.. But I am excited to see what I can do with it.. Although I think we might have that notion because of our age.. my father and grandfather's did the same.. "why buy something new when you can fix it and still use it" they used to say all the time.. I saw the sense in that and I do the same.. Thank Robert for doing this.. It makes me feel like I am not the only one still doing this!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I think it is a generational thing for sure mate - but interestingly enough - Luke - who is 20 - is as keen as mustard when it comes to this kind of thing

replies.author: @tinkmarshino
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering Well that is encouraging.. Maybe there is some hope for this old planet after all..

author: @tonysnyman9500

author: @stalbertjocelyn
text: Start with cleaning the carburator, we have ethanol in our gasoline in Canada, if you leave a engine sitting for more then a year the carburator clogs up solide. A ultrasonic bath takes care of it in a hurry. Don't forget to charge the field if you have no output. They often loose there residual magnetism from sitting too long. And like you said we need to STOP buying trow a ways . They are very tempting but don't last.. Have fun

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: awesome advice - cheers mate and thanks for taking the time

author: @AtlasReburdened
text: One of my greatest resources is junk yards. Just make friends with the owner or manager and they'll happily make deals that work for both of you. At my favorite ones it's simple. I just wave when I come in, take apart whatever I want to and toss the rubbish into it's appropriate pile, and pay for my goods priced out as clean, wire grade copper. It's sounds pricey with the time included but tools make that go quick and I still get amazing deals. I've gotten $300 centrifugal fans for $15-20 that just needed dents knocked out, loads of functioning computer hardware and other electronics for nothing, a $1000 industrial pump for $50 that just had stripped threads on a port. All kinds of goodies and it's win win.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I agree 100% mate- and let's be honest - it's just fun too lol

replies.author: @AtlasReburdened
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering It really is. It's probably my favorite way to be disappointed in humanity.

replies.author: @derekcartledge3057
replies.text: Check to see if the compressor is running also if it is running try warming the compressor up with a small blow lamp Until it is about 80° see if the compressor is not running there isn’t a lot you can do with it but you could try a swift smack with a hammer to start the compressor running sometimes works but not always good luck

author: @bernardstokes6550

author: @louisgandy4139
text: It's beyond the capacity of most to repurpose or repair Robert. Throwing it out is recycling at the very least. I also love repair and tinkering and am glad others are clueless cos I also get awesome things for free! Thanks for the video

replies.author: @JohnDavidDunlap
replies.text: When I was a teenager, my Uncle taught me an invaluable lesson. He had purchased an oil boiler for his house and everyone told him that he didn't know how to install one. He replied: "I will when I get done." Nobody knows how to do anything until they learn. There's a first time for everything. Pick reasonable goals and don't be afraid to learn new things. Your projects will naturally get bigger over time as you learn more and more. Everyone has to start somewhere. Don't be afraid of it.

replies.author: @aga5897
replies.text: @JohnDavidDunlap Yes, when you Have To, most people find that they Can.

replies.author: @lubbock2704
replies.text: @JohnDavidDunlap I completely agree with this. Though you don't have to be restricted to reasonable goals. Merely break the unreasonable ones down into many smaller, more reasonable goals :)

replies.author: @JohnDavidDunlap
replies.text: @Happymacer You're correct that you will never learn everything. But you can always learn more than you know! :)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I am sorry mate I don't really agree - I think everyone has the capacity - just not the desire and that's for lots of reasons. But if they lack skills then they can learn - or at least have the desire to do something if they lack the resources or time - then we would have a whole industry of folks who do have the skills repairing stuff in their own shops. I know I said 'off the soap box' but I don't think we should defend consumerism - I know that's not what you intended - but it is the effect I think - sorry mate - I don't mean to get at you - all the best
